restaurant.guide_title: Booking a Table at Mravel Lets You Relax and Enjoy Your Meal
restaurant.guide_intro_1: Get ready to enjoy more convenience when planning a meal with your family and friends using the
restaurant.guide_intro_online_booking: online restaurant booking service at Mravel
restaurant.guide_intro_2: . With just a few clicks, you can find and reserve a spot at the restaurants that best fit your schedule. All you need to do is
restaurant.guide_intro_steps: choose a restaurant, pick a time, and confirm your reservation
restaurant.guide_intro_3: – let Mravel handle the rest. Below are a few
restaurant.guide_intro_tips: tips for booking a table on Mravel
restaurant.guide_intro_4: to keep your experience running smoothly.
restaurant.guide_before_1_bold: List the key details about your meal
restaurant.guide_before_1_rest: , such as the number of guests, the dining date, your preferred time slot, the area, and your budget. This helps you quickly filter for suitable restaurants on Mravel.
restaurant.guide_before_2_bold: Choose the right time to book.
restaurant.guide_before_2_rest: On weekends or holidays, restaurants are often very crowded. Book a table in advance to avoid long waits or running out of available seats.
restaurant.guide_before_3_rest: On Mravel, you can view a sample menu, the location on the map, serving hours, photos of the space, and reviews from previous guests. Read them carefully to choose a place that fits your needs and style.
restaurant.guide_after_1_bold: Double-check the details in your booking confirmation
restaurant.guide_after_1_rest: sent via email or the Mravel app: restaurant name, address, time, number of guests, and any special notes (if any).
restaurant.guide_after_2_rest: to present when you arrive at the restaurant, making check-in faster and avoiding any mix-ups.
restaurant.guide_after_3_bold: Prepare the items you need to bring
restaurant.guide_after_3_rest: such as a phone logged into your Mravel account, a power bank, vouchers/offers (if any), and any essentials needed for the meal or trip.
restaurant.guide_after_4_bold: Arrive at the restaurant on time or a little earlier than your booked slot
restaurant.guide_after_4_rest: , especially during peak hours, to ensure a complete experience and avoid affecting service times.
restaurant.guide_after_5_rest: If you're satisfied, don't forget to leave a review on Mravel to give other customers helpful information and to help us keep improving your booking experience.

booking.table_suggestion_hint: Tip: the system can auto-suggest a table type based on the total number of guests.
booking.note_disclaimer: The note will be sent to the restaurant (fulfillment not guaranteed).
booking.table_booking_review_hint: Please review your contact details, time and table type before paying the deposit.
booking.policy_table_hold_text:
The table is held for the selected time slot.
- Hold time: 10–15 minutes (depending on the restaurant).
- If you arrive later than the hold time, the restaurant may release the table to serve other guests.
booking.policy_deposit_payment_text:
The deposit is used to hold the table and is deducted from your bill (subject to the restaurant's policy).
- The deposit is calculated by the number and type of tables.
- If the restaurant has special programs (set menu/party), fees may vary by time.
booking.policy_change_time_table_text:
You can contact the restaurant for adjustment support.
- Changing the time/table type depends on table availability.
- Some restaurants may require rebooking if changes are made too close to the time.
booking.policy_cancel_refund_text:
The refund policy depends on each restaurant.
Common examples:
- Cancel X hours in advance: deposit refunded (or partially refunded).
- Cancel close to the time or no-show: the deposit may be forfeited.
Please read the restaurant's description carefully or contact them directly to confirm.
booking.policy_contact_support_text:
If you need support (change time, change number of tables, allergy notes…), please call the restaurant directly.
When contacting, please provide:
- Booker's name
- Phone number
- Booking date/time
- Table type & number of tables
booking.policy_event_holiday_text:
During holidays/Tet or peak hours, the restaurant may apply:
- Mandatory set menu
- Maximum seating duration
- Higher deposit amount
The information will be shown on the detail page (if any).
booking.policy_parking_text:
Parking depends on each location.
- Some restaurants have their own parking lot or support nearby parking.
- Parking fees (if any) are collected by the parking lot and are not included in the deposit.
booking.policy_special_assistance_text:
If you need assistance (wheelchair, child seat, table near the window…), please note it when booking.
The restaurant will try to arrange it but cannot guarantee 100% as it depends on actual table availability.
booking.policy_summary_hold_text: The table is held for a short period. If you arrive later than the hold time, the restaurant may release the table.
booking.policy_summary_deposit_text: The deposit is calculated by the number and type of tables. Some restaurants have a refund policy that varies by time.
booking.hold_table_minutes_suffix: minutes from the selected time (depends on the restaurant).
booking.deposit_hold_note: The deposit is used to hold the table for your time slot. The remaining amount is paid at the restaurant.
booking.agree_terms_prefix: By continuing with the payment, you agree to the

booking.deposit_to_hold_table: Pay a deposit to hold the table for the selected time slot.

spotlight.heading: Stand out from the start

spotlight.fast_input_desc: A clean, structured form that helps you list your hotel/restaurant quickly and avoid mistakes.
spotlight.discoverable_desc: Your service appears in searches by location, price, ratings and travel itinerary suggestions.

hero.description: For hotel/guesthouse owners and restaurant owners. You can create a partner profile, list services, update information, and track performance — all in one clear management area, easy to demo business flows for reporting.
hero.benefit_support: Fast, simple and clear operational support
hero.signup_subtitle: Create a partner account to start listing your services on Mravel.
hero.feature_create_profile: Create a partner profile and list Hotel / Restaurant services.
hero.feature_pending_review: New or edited services will be in pending status awaiting Admin approval.
hero.feature_pause: Proactively pause when rooms/tables are full to avoid unwanted bookings.

q1.question: Why is my service in pending status after I publish it?
q1.answer: Because Mravel has a moderation process. New services or important edits move to pending for Admin review, ensuring the information shown to guests is accurate and consistent.

q2.question: While pending, can guests see my service?
q2.answer: While pending, the service is hidden from the user-facing pages to prevent guests from booking based on unapproved information. Once an administrator approves it, it is shown again automatically.

q3.question: Can I temporarily stop accepting guests?
q3.answer: Yes. You can temporarily lock the service when rooms/tables are full or maintenance is needed. Locking does not lose any data and you can reopen it at any time.

q4.question: When I delete a service, is it permanently removed?
q4.answer: Yes, once deleted the service is no longer visible to any user groups, but its history is retained so related bookings and itineraries are not affected.

safe.title: Operate with peace of mind
safe.subtitle: Clear statuses, controlled visibility
safe.bullet1: Creating/editing moves the service to pending for Admin review.
safe.bullet2: While awaiting approval, it can be hidden from users to avoid incorrect information.
safe.bullet3: You can lock/reopen a service when rooms/tables are full without losing data.
safe.desc: This setup makes it very easy to demonstrate in your report: role-based access (partner/admin), the service lifecycle (pending/approved/locked), and data consistency.

start.title: Publish a standard service
start.subtitle: Photos - description - amenities - operating hours
start.bullet1: A structured data entry form: name, address, description, photos, amenities.
start.bullet2: Hotels: you can expand room types, quantities, daily rates, and policies.
start.bullet3: Restaurants: opening hours, categories, price range, tables/deposit (if needed).
start.desc: The goal is to make a service page with 'enough information for guests to decide', while also having enough logic to present the moderation and update workflow.

reach.title: Increase your chances of getting guests
reach.subtitle: Appear in search & itinerary-based suggestions
reach.bullet1: Services appear in search results by location, price, ratings, and filters.
reach.bullet2: Plan-based suggestions: when a guest builds an itinerary, the system recommends suitable services by day.
reach.bullet3: Reach the right demand (following the itinerary), not just waiting for guests to find you.
reach.desc: This is the 'application value' highlight: partners benefit directly from Mravel's itinerary management feature.
